title: CNN Investigation: Children ~50% of Haiti's Gang Members, Raising GSF Operational Dilemma
detail: CNN published a major investigation on April 6 documenting that children make up approximately 50% of active gang members in Haiti — at least 302 children were 'recruited and used' by gangs in Port-au-Prince in 2024 alone, the vast majority in combat roles. The investigation raised urgent concerns about the approaching Gang Suppression Force (GSF) combat operations: multiple security experts warned that gang commanders would likely push child soldiers to the front lines when the GSF begins offensive operations, using them as disposable combatants and human shields. UNICEF's head of Haiti operations said they expected many children to exit gangs during GSF operations but expressed serious concern about casualties. CNN documented multiple pathways into gang membership for children: some surrendered by desperate or coerced families, others abducted from schools or streets, others drawn by promises of food and belonging amid the collapse of public services. Child gang recruitment increased 200% in 2025, per Amnesty International and UNICEF. The report underscored the strategic dilemma facing GSF commanders: aggressive operations risk killing children and triggering rights scrutiny, while restraint allows gangs to exploit child combatants indefinitely.

media.caption: CNN: Children make up half of Haiti's gangs; they are about to face a new foreign force

title: IMF Confirms Haiti Met All End-2025 Targets Under Staff-Monitored Program
detail: The International Monetary Fund announced on April 6 that Haitian authorities had met all program targets under the Third Review of Haiti's Staff-Monitored Program (SMP) as of end-December 2025. The IMF team led by Division Chief Camilo E. Tovar conducted a virtual review mission from March 23 to April 1, 2026. All quantitative targets were met, including international reserves accumulation, primary budget balance, revenue collection, limits on monetary financing, and social spending floors. The IMF noted reform implementation had 'advanced, although progress has been slower than anticipated in some areas, reflecting challenging security conditions, capacity constraints, and political uncertainty.' Haiti's authorities requested a program extension until June 19, 2027. SMPs are informal agreements intended to build a track record of economic management — a precondition for eventual access to upper credit tranche IMF financing. The confirmation provides a rare positive economic signal amid Haiti's continuing security and humanitarian crisis.
